mappings.notes: Private endpoint access is enabled and EKS access entries are codified for automation cluster-admin principals, but broad public endpoint access is accepted for dev and human operator access is not tied to Identity Center/RBAC.
mappings.gapSummary: Restrict public API CIDRs or move CI to private runners, then define human operator Identity Center mappings and least-privilege Kubernetes RBAC.

mappings.notes: IRSA is enabled and workloads do not declare AWS permissions, but application service accounts and token automount restrictions are not explicit and one trust policy lacks an audience condition.
mappings.gapSummary: Declare dedicated app service accounts, set automountServiceAccountToken intentionally, and complete IRSA trust conditions.

mappings.standardControlTitle: Limit add-on and controller cloud permissions
mappings.notes: Controllers use dedicated IRSA roles and scoped trust; ExternalDNS has narrow policies, while the load balancer controller still needs broad AWS API actions.
mappings.gapSummary: Document and test unavoidable wildcard scope and add guardrails for controller policy expansion.

mappings.notes: GitOps owns application resources and Argo CD separates platform/workload AppProjects, but the workload project still permits broad namespaced resource kinds and no admission/RBAC layer blocks privileged pods, high-privilege RBAC, or unauthorized service accounts.
mappings.gapSummary: Tighten workload AppProject resource allowances and add admission policy for privileged pods, RBAC escalation, and unauthorized identities.

mappings.standardControlTitle: Restrict public routing and administrative surfaces
mappings.notes: Gateway route attachment is namespace-scoped, but Argo CD and Grafana routes still require stronger operator authentication and source restrictions.
mappings.gapSummary: Add SSO, IP allowlists, and granular RBAC to admin interfaces.

mappings.standardControlTitle: Collect runtime logs and retain audit evidence
mappings.notes: Monitoring is deployed, but pod log forwarding, centralized audit retention, and reviewer-only audit access are not codified.
mappings.gapSummary: Define audit retention, immutable storage, and access boundaries for Kubernetes and deployment evidence.

mappings.notes: Vintage and Grafana Kubernetes Secrets are synced from AWS Secrets Manager through External Secrets Operator, but secrets still materialize as Kubernetes Secrets and rotation/restart/revocation workflows are manual.
mappings.gapSummary: Harden Kubernetes secret encryption/read access, reduce Terraform-originated secret state, and automate rotation, rollout, and revocation.
